giant cell tumor.default_prompt: a radiograph of giant cell tumor
descriptions: Expansile growth pattern with cortical breach in scapula.
Centrally located lytic lesion in distal femur.
Sunburst periosteal reaction around lytic lesion in radius.
Prominent soft tissue mass extending beyond diaphysis in humerus.
Irregular sclerotic margins surrounding lytic area in tibia.
Cortical thinning and bulging noted in proximal fibula.
Predominant lytic change with minimal sclerosis in ulna.
Eccentric lytic lesion at the metaphyseal region of the fibula.
Eccentric, pedunculated mass with ground-glass matrix.
Central, expansile lytic lesion with intact cortex.
Expansile lesion with a central sclerotic core in the femur.
Pedunculated mass at the metaphysis with a chondroid cap.
Unicentric, well-defined, juxtacortical lytic lesion.
Solitary, juxtacortical lytic area with surrounding sclerosis.
Broad-based pedicle with sclerotic margin at the tibia.
Cortical continuity preserved with a small, rounded exostosis.
Expansile lytic lesion in proximal tibia, cortical thinning.
Central sclerotic mass in distal radius, well-defined.
Sclerotic nodule at metaphyseal level, no periosteal reaction.
Ground-glass dense focus in calcaneus, short stalk.
Cortical-based mass in femoral neck, homogeneous density.
Ovoid sclerotic lesion in scapula, sharp margins.
Single sclerotic nodule in humeral diaphysis, smooth contour.
Medullary cavity involvement with sclerotic center in ulna.
Central lytic lesion with sunburst periosteal reaction in the distal femur.
Irregular cortical destruction with soft tissue mass extending into the adjacent muscle belly.
Expansile mixed lytic-sclerotic lesion at the metaphyseal-diaphyseal junction of the tibia.
Rapid bone expansion with loss of normal trabecular architecture in the pelvis.
Periosteal reaction forming a distinct sunburst pattern surrounding a sclerotic core.
Cortical breach with ground-glass matrix appearance in a central diaphyseal lesion.
Codman triangle present in a rapidly expanding lesion of the proximal humerus.
Multiple discrete sclerotic foci within a predominantly lytic area in the calcaneus.

simple bone cyst.default_prompt: a radiograph of simple bone cyst
descriptions: Central diaphyseal lytic lesion with fluid level.
Well-defined, round diaphyseal cyst with lucency.
Diaphyseal lesion has intact cortex; no breach.
Cystic area demonstrates ground-glass density.
Clear margins with no periosteal reaction present.
Medullary cavity shows expansile lytic lesion only.
Unicavitary defect in metaphysis lacks sclerotic rim.
Diaphyseal region exhibits unilocular lytic lesion without sclerosis.
Central lytic lesion with spiculated margin in the femoral head.
Expansile lytic lesion with cortical indentation in the proximal humerus.
Eccentrically placed sclerotic mass at the distal tibia.
Single, large, expansile lesion with eccentric positioning in the pelvis.
Multiple discrete foci of ground-glass density in the metaphysis.
Periosteal reaction present around a centrally located lytic lesion.
Large, soft tissue mass extending beyond the cortical boundary.
Ground-glass density within a well-defined cortically based lesion.
